hypothesis: Polymers with a moderate branching degree of 25–35% (i.e., 25–35% of monomers forming branch points) will exhibit at least 15% higher CO2 uptake (mmol g^-1) under DAC-relevant conditions (298 K, 0.4 mbar CO2, 50% RH) than both linear polymers and highly branched (>60%) analogues of equivalent overall amine density due to an optimal balance of accessible free volume and amine site availability.
